pet.name: Saw
pet.special_needs: Needs more oxygen than other fishes..
pet.characteristics: High-oxygen fish typically include active species such as goldfish, plecos, or fast-swimming cyprinids that require robust aeration and water circulation. Care involves maintaining dissolved oxygen levels through air stones, powerheads, or surface agitation; regular water changes (25–50% weekly); appropriate tank size relative to bioload; cool to moderate water temperatures depending on species; varied diet of quality pellets, vegetables, and occasional live foods; and consistent monitoring of pH, ammonia, and nitrite. These fish benefit from tank mates that tolerate similar conditions and may show stress (gasping, surface feeding) if oxygen is inadequate. Avoid overcrowding and stagnant water.
pet.species_profile_summary: Lifespan varies widely by specific species and water quality; high-oxygen fish thrive with excellent aeration, regular maintenance, and appropriate tank volume.

age_context.summary: Lifespan varies widely by specific species and water quality; high-oxygen fish thrive with excellent aeration, regular maintenance, and appropriate tank volume.

goal: Create a complete pet care plan with daily, weekly, monthly tasks, reminders, and safe condition-aware guidance.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: Older pets may benefit from routines that include medication reminders, slow feeding, and extra time for transitions. Owners should watch for changes in gait, confusion, appetite, and bathroom habits and report major changes to a veterinarian.

retrieved_passages.title: Kidney Diet and Hydration Support
retrieved_passages.content: Owners should follow the veterinary feeding plan already prescribed and avoid changing food or supplement amounts without veterinary guidance. If a pet refuses multiple meals or shows sudden decline, the safe action is to contact a veterinarian.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: Observation tasks are useful for special-needs pets because they create a record of appetite, water intake, energy, and bathroom habits. These tasks should be short, specific, and easy to complete consistently.

retrieved_passages.title: Medication Routine and Safety
retrieved_passages.content: Medication-related tasks in a home routine should focus on reminders, documentation, and observation, not dosage changes. A planning assistant can remind the owner to give medication exactly as already prescribed and to log whether the dose was completed.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: A senior pet plan should prioritize consistency over intensity. When time is limited, essential care such as feeding, hydration, medication reminders, and mobility support should be scheduled before optional enrichment.

raw_recommendations.name: Monitor oxygen levels and surface behavior
raw_recommendations.notes: Observe Saw for signs of adequate oxygenation such as normal swimming patterns and feeding behavior. Watch for gasping at the surface or lethargy, which may indicate insufficient dissolved oxygen.
raw_recommendations.rationale: High-oxygen fish require consistent aeration monitoring. Daily observation creates a record of energy and behavior changes, which is essential for special-needs pets and senior fish.

raw_recommendations.name: Check aeration equipment (air stone, powerhead, filter)
raw_recommendations.notes: Verify that air stones, powerheads, or surface agitation devices are functioning properly to maintain dissolved oxygen levels in the tank.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Robust aeration is critical for high-oxygen fish. Daily checks prevent equipment failure that could compromise water quality and oxygen availability.

raw_recommendations.name: Feed Saw with quality pellets or appropriate diet
raw_recommendations.notes: Provide measured portions of quality pellets or species-appropriate food. Monitor appetite and feeding response as part of daily observation routine.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Consistent feeding supports senior pet health and creates a routine. Appetite monitoring helps detect early signs of health changes in aging fish.

raw_recommendations.name: Perform 25–50% water change
raw_recommendations.notes: Change 25–50% of tank water weekly to maintain dissolved oxygen levels, remove ammonia and nitrite buildup, and support overall water quality for high-oxygen fish.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Regular water changes are essential for high-oxygen fish species and prevent stagnant water conditions. This supports Saw's special oxygen requirements and senior health.

raw_recommendations.name: Test water parameters (pH, ammonia, nitrite)
raw_recommendations.notes: Check pH, ammonia, and nitrite levels weekly to ensure water conditions remain safe and support adequate dissolved oxygen retention.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Water parameter monitoring is critical for high-oxygen fish and helps detect early signs of tank imbalance that could stress a senior fish.

raw_recommendations.name: Inspect tank for overcrowding and stagnant areas
raw_recommendations.notes: Verify that tank size is appropriate for Saw's bioload, that water circulation is adequate, and that no dead zones with poor water flow exist.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Overcrowding and stagnant water compromise oxygen availability. Weekly inspection ensures the tank environment remains suitable for a high-oxygen fish.

raw_recommendations.name: Document appetite, energy, and behavior changes
raw_recommendations.notes: Keep a brief log of Saw's appetite, activity level, and any behavioral changes. Report major changes to a veterinarian.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Consistent documentation of senior pet behavior helps detect early health changes and supports veterinary communication if needed.

raw_recommendations.name: Clean or replace air stone and check filter media
raw_recommendations.notes: Monthly inspection and cleaning of air stones and filter media to ensure optimal aeration and water circulation performance.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Equipment maintenance prevents degradation of aeration capacity, which is essential for high-oxygen fish and senior pet health.
